description: Companies discovered during Wave 1 supplier/client research - Wave 2 data collection COMPLETE

priorityLegend.high: Mentioned by 3+ seed companies, critical supply chain node
priorityLegend.low: Mentioned by 1 seed company or indirect relationship

discoveredCompanies.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC)
discoveredCompanies.notes: World's largest semiconductor foundry. Customer of almost all Japanese equipment/materials makers.

discoveredCompanies.name: Samsung Electronics
discoveredCompanies.notes: Major memory and foundry player. Key customer for deposition, test, and inspection equipment.

discoveredCompanies.name: Intel Corporation
discoveredCompanies.notes: Major IDM. Largest installed base of Lasertec EUV inspection tools (14 units).

discoveredCompanies.name: SK Hynix
discoveredCompanies.notes: Major memory maker. Customer for HBM-related equipment.

discoveredCompanies.name: Apple Inc.
discoveredCompanies.notes: Direct customer for Kioxia NAND flash. Indirect customer via TSMC.

discoveredCompanies.name: Western Digital Corporation
discoveredCompanies.notes: Manufacturing partner with Kioxia. Joint venture in Yokkaichi and Kitakami fabs.

discoveredCompanies.name: Shin-Etsu Chemical Co., Ltd.
discoveredCompanies.notes: World's largest silicon wafer supplier. Supplies materials to equipment makers.

discoveredCompanies.name: SUMCO Corporation
discoveredCompanies.notes: Major silicon wafer supplier. Together with Shin-Etsu, holds 90% of wafer market.

discoveredCompanies.name: ASML Holding N.V.
discoveredCompanies.notes: EUV lithography monopoly. Lasertec inspection tools are co-dependent with ASML systems. HOYA mask blanks are required for EUV.

discoveredCompanies.name: Kyocera Corporation
discoveredCompanies.notes: Supplies ceramic components and packages for semiconductor equipment.

discoveredCompanies.name: NVIDIA Corporation
discoveredCompanies.notes: Fabless. Uses TSMC for manufacturing. Drives demand for test equipment.

internalRelationshipsNoted.notes: Kioxia likely uses Kokusai Electric batch deposition equipment for 3D NAND
Tokyo Seimitsu and DISCO compete in dicing. DISCO 80%, Tokyo Seimitsu 10% market share.
